title: Chimichurri Steak with Roasted Broccolini
description: Grilled steak finished with bright, garlicky chimichurri alongside oven-roasted broccolini. Everything comes together in 45 minutes with coordinated grill and oven timing.

ingredients.name: steaks, 1.25–1.5 inches thick (ribeye, NY strip, or sirloin)

instructions.text: Make the chimichurri: combine parsley, garlic, salt, red pepper flakes, red wine vinegar, and olive oil. Mix well and let sit at room temperature. Can be made hours ahead.
Preheat oven to 425°F and preheat grill to high (450–550°F). Remove steaks from the refrigerator. Season both sides generously with salt and pepper and let sit at room temperature.

Toss broccolini with olive oil and salt. Spread on a sheet pan in a single layer and place in the oven. Set a timer for 15–18 minutes. Broccolini should be slightly charred — that bitterness balances the steak fat and chimichurri.

Place steaks on the hot grill, lid closed. For 1.25–1.5 inch thick steaks: 4–5 minutes first side, flip, 4–5 minutes second side. Pull at 125°F for medium-rare, 130°F for medium. Do not cut before resting.
Transfer steaks to a plate and rest 10 minutes. Do not skip — cutting early causes significant juice loss.
Remove broccolini from oven. Squeeze lemon over if desired.
Slice steak across the grain. Spoon chimichurri generously over the top. Serve immediately with broccolini alongside.

notes:
Chimichurri improves as it sits — make it up to a day ahead and refrigerate, bringing it back to room temperature before serving.

Grill times assume a 1.25–1.5 inch thick steak. Thinner steaks (under 1 inch) need 3 minutes per side; thicker cuts (over 1.5 inches) may need a reverse sear or longer indirect time.

The slight char on the broccolini is intentional — don't pull it too early.
